mnemonics: TLC PREP - Traversal: In, Pre, Post. Levels: Breadth-first. CRUD: Insert, Delete, Search. Extra: Height/Depth. Properties: Balance, Size.
keyPoints: Tree traversal (Inorder, Preorder, Postorder) processes nodes systematically
Insertion and deletion operations must maintain the tree's structural integrity
Tree properties like height, depth, and balance factor are crucial for efficiency
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle edge cases like empty trees or single-node trees
Updating parent/child pointers incorrectly during insertion or deletion, causing broken links
Assuming a binary tree is always balanced, leading to incorrect time complexity calculations
